name: TAO Range Failure Reacceptance v1
thesis: TAO 4h is the best-fit primary lane for a range-failure reversal after a multi-bar breakout loses acceptance and re-enters balance. The edge should come from trapped continuation flow unwinding back into value, not from trying to pick tops and bottoms before failure is visible.
invalidation_condition: Abort if range-failure setups remain too sparse, if reacceptance does not rotate price back through value, or if the lane trends too cleanly for reversal logic.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price extends beyond a local range for more than one bar, loses acceptance, and rotates back through near-term value as trapped continuation participants unwind.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: With chop still dominating the recent regime mix, failed continuation should remain more common than perfect trend persistence. Re-entry into balance is broader and denser than rare exhaustion candles.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is suitable because false acceptance after visible expansion is clearer on a volatile asset, while 4h bars still preserve enough structure to define balance loss and reacceptance.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If TAO enters persistent trend-up rather than failed continuation, reversal entries will be steamrolled.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If the reversal participates but exits too early, widen the back-half exit before tightening the reacceptance trigger.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; extension context and reacceptance are sequential
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed with broad multi-bar extension plus value re-entry instead of exact trap candles

long: Trail remaining 20% behind EMA_20 with 0.4 * ATR_14 buffer
short: Trail remaining 20% behind EMA_20 with 0.4 * ATR_14 buffer
